mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to discount future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and supply chain throughput improvements, creating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 during periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy and elevated inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and increases discount rates, resulting in negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 60% of observations when both inflation and interest rates remain above their specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth above the 2% threshold confirms robust macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive quarterly S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of periods where GDP growth exceeds the 2% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand household disposable income and consumer spending capacity, leading to higher corporate revenues and positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The payroll employment series shows zero predictive power with Rank IC equal to 0 for 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth consistently trails retail sales growth, supply constraints emerge that force price increases and margin compression, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive when the production-to-sales growth differential falls below -1.5% for three consecutive months.
